note: Per-slide audio-briefing speaker notes for the USSFCU CEO Executive Briefing (Presentation Mode). Highlights only — spoken like a live meeting, not a read-out of the slide. Numbers are spelled for natural text-to-speech. Keyed by slide id (mirrors SLIDE_IDS in presentationChat.js). Slide 1 opens with the greeting.

narration.situational: Good morning, Tim. Here's your executive briefing. This morning's headline is a liquidity watch — loan demand is outpacing deposit growth. Loan-to-share has reached eighty-four percent, a five-year high, with loans up more than eleven percent against deposits at just four. If this holds, liquidity tightens below the board's target by late in the fourth quarter.
narration.evidence: Let's ground that number. The eighty-four percent reconciles across three systems — net loans of one-point-two-nine billion from Symitar, shares of one-point-five-four billion from the general ledger, and a ninety-day pipeline of three hundred twelve million. One figure, three sources, ninety-four percent confidence, traceable from core to board brief.
narration.trajectory: Here's the trajectory if we don't act. On the current path, loan-to-share crosses the board's eighty-five percent ceiling in September and reaches eighty-nine by December. This is the base case, with no intervention — and it's why we're bringing this to the September board now, not later.
narration.assurance: Before the recommendations, a word on trust. Every figure here comes from this morning's validated data load at six-oh-four. The trust layer reconciled all sources, caught the one late feed, and every headline number traces back to source — board and examiner ready.
narration.members: It's worth remembering what those deposits are: our members' savings. As Ruth, a member since 1998, put it — USSFCU behaves as a credit union ought to. That mission is why the deposit strategy matters, and it's recognized: CEO of the Year, Top Workplaces, and a Bauer five-star rating.
narration.resolution: So, three options for the board. My recommendation is option one — a targeted deposit campaign across the D-M-V and Senate alumni: low cost, two to four weeks, and roughly eighty-five million in new deposits. We pair it with option two, a standby funding line, as a buffer. Option three, slowing loan pacing, stays in reserve.
narration.nextSteps: And here's what I'm asking of the board. One — approve the deposit campaign before month-end. Two — commission the funding-line review within five business days. Three — we brief the full board in September. Clear actions, clear owners. Thank you.
